titleStmt.title: SII 15.231: original edition by P. B. Desai, Dines Chandra Sircar and G. S. Gai
No. 231. (A.R. No. 248 of 1926.) AMMINBHĀVI, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a hero-stone set up in the Kalamēśvara temple.

This is dated the 18th regnal year of the chief, Śōbhakṛit, Chaitra śu. 11, Tuesday. The cyclic year corresponds to 1183-84 A.D. But the details of the date are irregular. Chaitra śu. 11 of that year corresponded to March 6, the weekday being Sunday. Śivachitta Permāḍi of the Kadamba family of Goa who also bore the title Tribhuvanamalla, is known to have commenced his reign from 1147 A.D. Thus the regnal year cited would be too low for his reign.
It describes an officer and records his death in a fight. The epigraph is worn out and no other details can be made out.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī kādaṁba śrī sivacita dēva tribhuvanamalladēvavarusada 18 neya śōbhakritu saṁvatsarada cyayitra su 11 maṁgaḷavā
radaṁdu svasti samastapraśastisahitaṁ śrīmanmahāpasāytaṁ paramavisvāsi ni¨rmmūlanuṁ ya
visama hayārūḍha pravuḍarēkhārēvantanuṁ baṁndujanaciṁtāmaṇiyu śritajana kalpavrikṣa Aribiruda nāyka vēśyā
bhujaṁga parabaḷabaḷadō¨rbbaḷaga¨rbbadiṁ tārātināyka bbhājana maṁtraprasiddhanuṁ dakṣa

pratipāḷanuṁ yanāgata vikramādityanuṁ vājā siddhiya
tribhuvanamalladēva rājyabharabhāradhavurēya dikkuṁjaranuṁ śrī mūlasthānadēvara divyaśrīpādapadmārādhakanuṁ
parabaḷasādhakanumappa māyenāykanu rsara besadiṁ kuṁbisaya bīrarsana mēle
geludu sva¨rggavaneydidanu . cchaladiṁ saṇasutippara talegaḷaṁ taridoṭṭi
baladoḷu ṭṭanahita gharaṭṭaṁ . The rest of the record is worn out.
Digital edition of SII 15.231 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
